summary: Decompiled sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s into C, replacing the 1477-line assembly file with src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c and adding mtx prototypes/header coverage. The work covered matrix inverse, inverse-concat, Euler/scale/translation extraction and construction helpers, vector/matrix alloc init helpers, and related PSMTX/PSVEC prototypes. The PR also adjusted build wiring and attempted Mac-friendly Makefile changes, but review pushed back to keep sha1sum for Windows/Linux and make shasum macOS-specific. Review also emphasized using math.h facilities/static standard-library behavior rather than duplicating macros or writing fake code.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s and added src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c with C implementations for the baselib matrix routines.

Added/updated prototypes for mtx functions such as func_80379310, HSD_MtxInverseConcat, func_80379A20, func_80379C24, func_80379F6C, func_80379F88, func_8037A120, func_8037A230, func_8037A250, func_8037A43C, func_8037A54C, HSD_VecInitAllocData, and HSD_MtxInitAllocData.

Added PSMTX and PSVEC declarations in include/functions.h for matrix/vector primitives used by the C implementation, including PSMTXIdentity, PSMTXCopy, PSMTXConcat, PSMTXQuat, PSVECMag, PSVECNormalize, PSVECDotProduct, and PSVECCrossProduct.
Updated obj_files.mk/build wiring so mtx builds from C instead of the old assembly object.
Touched dependent fighter, item, and jobj files to align with the new mtx declarations and include structure.

Modified Makefile program selection for CPP and SHA1SUM in an attempt to support macOS; review indicated the SHA1SUM change should be macOS-specific rather than replacing sha1sum for all non-Windows hosts.

The PR body says it began as draft because the author wanted to remove fakematches, especially in the determinant function; an issue comment notes there was still one nonmatching function at one point.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated 3x4-matrix determinant calculation into HSD_CalcDeterminantMatrix3x4, making inverse-related routines easier to compare against the assembly.
Handled source/destination aliasing explicitly in inverse routines by copying to a temporary Mtx before writing when src/inv overlaps dest, mirroring assembly behavior and avoiding clobbering inputs.
Used existing Dolphin/PS matrix and vector primitives such as PSMTXCopy, PSMTXIdentity, PSMTXConcat, PSMTXQuat, PSVECNormalize, PSVECScale, and PSVECDotProduct where the assembly called out to them.
Kept the numerical edge cases visible: determinant epsilon checks, FLOAT_MIN checks, identity fallback for singular inverse, and copy-through fallback for failed inverse-concat.
Used decomp.me scratch links during review for matching discussion, including a reviewer-provided scratch for src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c.
decomp_lessons: For math-heavy decompilation, preserve operation order aggressively; the diff comment says func_80379A20 needed stores in a different order than func_80379310 for matching.
When decompiling matrix routines, aliasing paths are often part of the observable assembly shape. Use temporary matrices and conditional PSMTXCopy calls when src/dest can overlap.
Do not duplicate standard-library math macros/functions in headers to force matches. Review explicitly said to use math.h and not write fake code.
Static variables used by standard math implementations can still be emitted/initialized for a file, so replacing hand-written fake sqrtf-style header code with math.h can be compatible with matching.
Cross-platform build changes should be narrowly scoped. Review preferred keeping sha1sum on Windows/Linux and using shasum only in a macOS-specific branch.
Leaving a NON_MATCHING function or known nonmatch should be clearly isolated and called out; the author explicitly warned about one remaining nonmatching function during the PR.
naming_conventions: Known baselib functions keep the HSD_ prefix, e.g. HSD_MtxInverseConcat, HSD_VecInitAllocData, and HSD_MtxInitAllocData.
Dolphin SDK-style matrix/vector primitives keep PSMTX and PSVEC prefixes, e.g. PSMTXCopy, PSMTXQuat, PSVECMag, and PSVECCrossProduct.
Unidentified functions remained address-based func_8037xxxx names rather than speculative semantic names.
Helper names in the submitted code used HSD_CalcDeterminantMatrix3x4 and calcVal; evidence does not show a reviewer-approved final semantic naming pass for all helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline helpers for determinant and bitwise absolute value to control generated floating-point code shape.
Matched singular-matrix branches with epsilon comparisons and exact fallback calls such as PSMTXIdentity or PSMTXCopy.
Preserved temporary variable ordering in HSD_MtxInverseConcat, including many explicit tempN locals, to reproduce floating-point scheduling and register lifetimes.
Matched in-place matrix operations by branching to a stack temporary Mtx and copying back to dest.
Used NON_MATCHING around at least one function in the diff excerpt, consistent with the PR discussion that one function remained nonmatching at a point in review.

review_feedback.feedback: Provided a decomp.me scratch link for matching work: https://decomp.me/scratch/h8te3.
Asked to use math.h instead of duplicating macros/functions in the header.
Clarified that standard-library static variables can still be initialized in the file and said: Do not write fake code.
Questioned changing sha1sum because it is part of GNU coreutils and should remain the same on Windows/Linux.
Accepted that macOS uses shasum, but preferred keeping sha1sum on Windows/Linux and making shasum mac-specific.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed func_80379310 or other mtx func_8037xxxx symbols to semantic HSD_Mtx names.
Check whether func_8037A54C or the one nonmatching function from this PR was later matched and removed from NON_MATCHING.
Search later build-system PRs for the final macOS-specific sha1sum/shasum Makefile handling.
Search for later cleanup of mtx.h math.h usage and removal of fake sqrtf or duplicated math macros.
Inspect dependent fighter/item/jobj changes to see which mtx prototypes or include paths were required downstream.
